title: Luffy Stands Up to the Bandits
description: On pages 24–27, this part follows the Romance Dawn flashback and focuses on how Luffy’s pirate dream is formed through Shanks, Higuma, and the straw hat promise.
scene: Luffy’s loyalty to Shanks pushes him into danger as the bandits turn their attention toward him.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: It's not here!!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: Huh?!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: The gum- gum fruit we took from that enemy ship!!!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: Luffy! You didn't eat that fruit, did you!?

dialogue.speaker: Higuma
dialogue.text: Yeah...I ate it for Dessert!!

dialogue.speaker: Higuma
dialogue.text: "I didn't taste all that GREAT, though...

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: That was the gum- gum fruit!!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: The fruit of the devil, it's one of the secret treea- 1 sures of the sea!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: If you eat it, your entire body becomes like rubber! And you will never be able to swim for the rest of your life!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: WhaaaaAAABAAT!!?

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: IDIOT!!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Fish monger

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Smirk!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hey, Mr. Fish monger!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Give me some fish!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: HEY, RUBBER BOY! WHY ARE YOU IN SUCH A GOOD MOOD LATELY!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: THE PIRATES SET SAIL WITHOUT YOU TODAY, YOU KNOW?

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: And you won't be able to swim for the rest of your life.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: IT DOESN'T MATTER. IF I SINK LIKE ANANCHOR, THEN I'LL JUST BE A PIRATE THAT NEVER FALLS OVERBOARD!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'M GLAD I ATE THE GUM-GUM DEVIL FRUIT...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Look at what I can do!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: YOU THINK BEING GAWKED AT BECAUSE YOU'RE A FREAK IS A GREAT THING, EH?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: WELL, THIS VILLAGE DOESN'T NEED ANY MORE IDIOTS, SONNY!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Good morning, Mr. Mayor!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: FOR THE LAST TIME, LIFFY, I WON'T ALLOW YOU TO BECOME A PIRATE!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: IT'D MAKE THE VILLAGE LOOK BAD!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: NOW, THE CAPTAIN'S NOT SUCH A BAD FELLOW, BUT YOU STAY AWAY FROM THOSE PIRATES!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: THE PIRATES HAVE BEEN OUT AT SEA FOR A LONG TIME.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: Do you miss them, Luffy?

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: After the way those mountain bandits humulated them?! Not at all!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I REALLY MIS- JUDGED THOSE GUYS!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Thought THEY WERE BREVE AND TOUGH... BUT THEY'RE REALLY JUST A BUNCH OF WIMPS.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: ARE YOU SURE ABOUT THAT?

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: MAYBE SOME- TIMES IT TAKES MORE COURAGE NOT TO FIGHT.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You just wouldn't understand, makino.

summary: Luffy Stands Up to the Bandits is the main story beat of this section. The dialogue and reactions show how the chapter’s conflict develops, while the scene keeps the focus on the characters’ choices rather than technical OCR structure.
